reset.description: Activates feedback when set to 0. Disables feedback when set to 1. When disabled, the Feedback CHOP passes thru the data connected to its input.

rateoption.description: Use this menu to adjust the sample rate of the loaded channels.

type.description: There are seven types of filters:

effect.description: The extent to which the filter affects the channel (0 - not at all, 1 - maximum effect).

width.description: The amount of surrounding samples used in the calculation of the current sample. It is expressed in the Units.

widthunit.description: Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

spike.description: For the De-spike filter type, this is the amount that a sample can differ from its neighbours without being considered a spike.

ramptolerance.description: When using a Ramp Preserve filter, if the input value deviates from the current output ramp value by this much, then the ramp will reset to the new...

ramprate.description: When using a Ramp Preserve filter, this is the rate that the CHOP 's output channel will increase. Only if the input channel value deviates from th...

passes.description: The number of times the filter is applied to the channel.

cutoff.description: Decrease it if slow speed jitter is a problem.

speedcoeff.description: Increase if high speed lag is a problem.

slopecutoff.description: Avoids high derivative bursts caused by jitter.

slopedownreset.description: When On resets (bypasses) the filter effect when the downward slope exceeds the Slope Down Max value to the right.

slopedownmax.description: Set the maximum value of the slope downwards beyond which the filter resets when the toggle to the left is On.

slopeupreset.description: When On resets (bypasses) the filter effect when the upward slope exceeds the Slope Up Max value to the right.

slopeupmax.description: Set the maximum value of the slope upwards beyond which the filter resets when the toggle to the left is On.

reset.description: When On resets (bypasses) the filter effect.

resetpulse.description: Instantly resets the filter effect.

filterpersample.description: Applies the filter to each sample of the channel instead of across the whole channel. Useful for working with multi-sample channels.

handsbounds.description: Enable the position and bounds channels (u, v, width, height) for hands.

hands.description: Enable the position channels (u, v) for all hand tracking points.

edgethreshold.description: Controls the sensitivity of edge detection. Use the "Output Edges" mode to see it in action.

maxsearchsteps.description: How many horizontal and vertical search steps are done when looking for edges.

source.description: Source of the webpage, which can be an address via URL or File, or data from a DAT

url.description: Uniform Resource Locator or the address of the web page

dat.description: Text to be loaded into a webpage

reloadsrc.description: Reload source DAT , URL or File.

reload.description: Reloads the current page, the same as refreshing a web browser.

resetcount.description: Reset the counter for the number of times the webpage has been updated (available via an Info CHOP ).

updatewhenloaded.description: Only shows the web page when it is full loaded.

alwayscook.description: Cook every frame if on. If off, the Web Render TOP will cook for a 10 additional frames after the last update received to maintain continuity.

transparent.description: Loads the webpage with a transparent background. This option will restart the browser process.

audio.description: Let the browser process play audio if the web page contains audio. This option will restart the browser process.

mediastream.description: Allows the web render instance to use the mic and the camera.

redirectpopups.description: Instead of ignorning popups, the Web Render TOP switches to the url for the popup.

maxrenderrate.description: Sets the maximum frame rate the page will be rendered at. Can be higher than 60 but does not guarantee a frame rate.

numbuffers.description: Sets the maximum number of cached images

dpiscale.description: Sets the DPI scaling factor for the rendered webpage. Default expression matches the DPI Scale for monitor 0 of TouchDesigner.

autorestart.description: Automatically restart the browser process if it died.

autorestartpulse.description: Triggers the Restart immediately on button release (button-up). This can be accessed in python using the pulse() method.

active.description: Controls whether this TOP is retrieving image data from the device. The primary ZED TOP must also be active to receive data.

zedtop.description: The name of the primary ZED TOP that is configuring the camera. The primary TOP controls which camera the select TOP receives data from.

perspective.description: Choose between Left or Right camera.

image.description: Selects between the Color, Depth, Confidence, Disparity, Normals, Point Cloud or Spatial Texture modes.

maxdepth.description: Sets the maximum depth in meters.

toocloseval.description: For depth pixels that are too close to resolve, this pixel value will be output instead.

toofarval.description: For depth pixels that are too far to resolve, this pixel value will be output instead.

unknownval.description: For depth pixels whose depth can not be determined, output this value instead.

rerange.description: Enabling this will remap pixel values to 0-1.

mirrorimage.description: Flips the image in the y-axis.

active.description: When set to 1 the TOP captures the image stream from the camera.

inputsource.description: Select which input type to use between USB, recorded SVO file, or network streaming

camera.description: Selects which ZED camera to use.

file.description: The path and name of the recorded SVO file to load. SVO and SVO2 playback is supported.

streamip.description: The IP address of the streaming camera.

streamport.description: The port of the streaming camera.

initialize.description: (pulse) Initializes the SVO playback: sets the SVO position to zero.

start.description: (pulse) Start is the signal to commence the SVO playback.

play.description: SVO plays when 1, SVO stops when 0.

cue.description: Jumps to Cue Point when set to 1.

cuepoint.description: Set the SVO position in the SVO playback as a point to jump to.

cueunit.description: Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, and Seconds.

perspective.description: Choose between Left or Right camera.

image.description: Selects between the Color, Depth, Confidence, Disparity, Normals, Point Cloud, Spatial Texture, or Mask modes.

attenuated.description: Turn on this checkbox to enable distance-based attenuation of the light.

attenuationstart.description: The distance from the light source where the light attenuation begins.

attenuationend.description: The distance from the light source where the light attenuation ends (i.e., no light radiates beyond this point).

attenuationexp.description: Controls how the light fades off between the Attenuation Start and End points.

projmapextendu.description: Sets the extend conditions for the Projector Map texture.

projmapextendv.description: Sets the extend conditions for the Projector Map texture.

projmapextendw.description: Sets the extend conditions for the Projector Map texture.

projmapmode.description: Specify how the projection map is applied

projangle.description: Specifies the cone angle spread of the projector map, similar to how the Cone Angle parameter works for Cone Lights.

frontfacelit.description: Controls how the polygon's normal is used to light the front face of the polygon. For more information refer to the Two-Sided Lighting article.

backfacelit.description: Controls how the polygon's normal is used to light the back face of the polygon. For more information refer to the Two-Sided Lighting article.

shadowtype.description: Sets the type of shadows cast by the light.

shadowcasters.description: The Geometry COMPs that will cast shadows from this light.

lightsize.description: Controls the size of the source light when using Soft or Custom shadows.

maxshadowsoftness.description: Fine tuning for the shadow's software when using Soft or Custom shadows.

filtersamples.description: Controls how many samples to look up into the shadow map for each pixel when doing soft shadows.

searchsteps.description: Controls how many steps to take to search for occlusion when doing soft shadows.

polygonoffsetfactor.description: Adds an offset to the Z value that depends on how sloped the surface is to the viewer when rendering the shadow map. Helps avoid z-fighting artifacts.

polygonoffsetunits.description: Adds a constant offset to the Z value when rendering the shadow map. Helps avoid z-fighting artifacts.

shadowresolution.description: The resolution of the shadow's texture map used for the calculation.

shadowmap.description: The path to a TOP used for the light's shadow map. See also Rendering Shadows .

projection.description: A pop-up menu lets you choose the projection type.

aspectcorrect.description: Keeps the aspect ratio of the view correct when using the light as a camera to look through.

orthowidth.description: Only active if Orthographic is chosen from the Projection pop-up menu. This specifies the width of the orthographic projection.

useconeforfov.description: If the light is set to Cone Light type, enabling this option sets the FOV using the Cone Angle and Cone Delta parameters on the Light parameter page.

viewanglemethod.description: This menu determines which method is used to define the camera's angle of view.

fov.description: The field of view (FOV) angle is the angular extend of the scene imaged by the camera.

aperture.description: This value relates to the area through which light can pass for the camera.

near.description: This control allows you to designate the near clipping planes. Geometry closer to the lens than these distances will not be visible.

far.description: This control allows you to designate the far clipping planes. Geometry further away from the lens than these distances will not be visible.

actors.description: The Actor COMPs to include in the simulation. These actors cannot already be a part of another Solver COMP .

forces.description: The Force COMPs to include in the simulation. Only force fields are supported in Flex.

gravity.description: Gravity applied to all actors in the simulation in m/s^2. Gravity is applied to actors irrespective of their mass.

init.description: Reset all bodies to their initial state (ie. position, orientation, velocity). This will not begin stepping through the simulation, it will only in...

start.description: Initialize the simulation and run it (begin stepping).

play.description: Play the simulation. Will step through the simulation when toggled on, but will be paused when toggled off.

rate.description: The sample rate of the simulation. The sample rate affects the timestep, which is 1/rate

substeps.description: Number of substeps to take during one step of the simulation.

iterations.description: Number of iterations in each substep.

alwayssim.description: When enabled the simulation will always step forward.

bounds.description: Enable simulation boundaries. These can either be set through a bounding box or individual planes.

boundmode.description: Use either a Bounding Box SOP specified below or individual planes (also specified below) to set the boundaries.

bbox.description: The SOP used to calculate the bounding box.

plane.description: Sequence of planes to add to the solver

plane0r.description: Rotate the default plane. Default plane is a +Z XY plane (same as Grid SOP ).

plane0t.description: Translate the default plane. Default plane is a +Z XY plane (same as Grid SOP ).

radius.description: Radius of the particles in the simulation. Note: This is used by all Actor's in the simulation.

dfriction.description: The force of friction between a moving particle and a static shape.

sfriction.description: The force of friction between a non-moving particle and a static shape.

rest.description: The coefficient of restitution for particles.

adhesion.description: How strongly particles stick to surfaces/shapes they hit.

sleepthresh.description: Particles with a velocity less than this threshold will be considered non-moving.

maxspeed.description: The magnitude of particle velocity will be clamped to this value.

maxaccel.description: The magnitude of particle acceleration will be clamped to this value.

diss.description: Damps particle velocity based on how many particle contacts it has.

damping.description: Viscous drag force. Applies a force proportional and opposite to the particle velocity.

cohesion.description: Controls how strongles particles hold to each other.

surftension.description: Controls how strongly particles attempt to minimize surface area.

viscosity.description: Smoothes particle velocities using XSPH viscosity.

buoyancy.description: A gravity scale for fluid particles.

colldist.description: The distance particles maintain when colliding against shapes.

scollmargin.description: Increases the particle radius during contact finding against shapes.

smoothing.description: Controls the strength of Laplacian smoothing in particles for rendering.

vortconf.description: Increases vorticity by applying rotational forces to particles.

anisoscale.description: Control how much anisotropys is present in resulting ellipsoids for rendering.

anisomin.description: Clamp the anisotropy scale to this fraction of the radius.

anisomax.description: Clamp the anisotropy scale to this fraction of the radius.

diffuse.description: When enabled, diffuse particles will be created in the simulation. Diffuse particle position/velocity can be fetched using the Nvidia Flex TOP .

maxdiffuse.description: The maximum number of diffuse particles that can exist in a simulation simultaneously. Note: if the maximum is too low or the diffuse lifetime too ...

diffthresh.description: Particles with kinetic energy + divergence above this threshold will spawn new diffuse particles

diffbuoy.description: A gravity scale for diffuse particles.

diffdrag.description: Scales the force that diffuse particles receive in direction of neighbouring fluid particles.

diffball.description: The number of neighbours below which a diffuse particle is considered ballistic.

difflife.description: Time in seconds that a diffuse particle will live for after being spawned.

closedu.description: Enable closed geometry in U direction.

angleu.description: The angle the tube covers along U.

attrclass.description: Makes the POP operate on point attributes, vertex attributes or primitive attributes where applicable.

inputattrscope.description: Input's attributes you want to affect within the chosen attribute class, or attribute components.

weightattr.description: Specifies the scope of the attribute that stores weight values

paxis.description: Primary transform axis of the twist.

p.description: The pivot point for the transform rotates and scales.

rolloff.description: Rolloff factor on the twist operation.

overrideautoattr.description: Whether to override the kind of attribute automatically created based on the POP input and parameters. Allows to specify manually the type and numb...

attrtype.description: The output attribute's data type, default float.

attrnumcomps.description: The number of components in the new custom attribute.

attrdefaultval.description: Default values of the output attribute components if they cannot be computed.

active.description: Extra pass to free unused GPU memory.

outputmode.description: Whether to output a mesh or a point cloud.

connectivity.description: Determines whether and how to connect the points.

initialize.description: Clears the extracted geometry and resets spatial mapping.

play.description: Enable playback.

donepulse.description: Will immediately go to the done state.

maxmemory.description: Sets the maximum memory used for spatial mapping.

normals.description: When enabled, the extracted geometry will have normals.

color.description: Output color information as an attribute.

filter.description: The greater the filtering, the more the resulting mesh is smoothed, small holes are filled, and small blobs of non-connected triangles are deleted.

overridecamera.description: When on, overrides the View settings from the selected camera object.

viewanglemethod.description: Sets the method to convert the depth TOP .

fov.description: Determines the camera's field of view angle.

center.description: The location of the center of the camera for the depth projection.

deletenear.description: Enable removal of points that are close to the camera.

depthnear.description: Depth of the near clipping plane.

deletefar.description: Enable removal of points that are far from the camera.

depthfar.description: Depth of the far clipping plane.

type.description: Attractor type
